title: 11 Herbal Creams For Acid Reflux
intro_desc: Herbal creams can provide relief for acid reflux symptoms by soothing the stomach and esophagus. However, herbal teas are often more effective at relieving acid reflux because they can be easily absorbed into the body through the digestive system. When you drink an herbal tea, the active ingredients are released into your bloodstream and can help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation. This can help to neutralize stomach acid and prevent it from flowing back up into the esophagus.  Some herbs that are commonly used to relieve acid reflux symptoms include Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation. It's also been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, which can help to prevent damage from stomach acid. Another herb that can help with acid reflux is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and calm the digestive system. It's also been shown to help reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of acid reflux. Finally, Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, can also be helpful in relieving acid reflux symptoms. Peppermint oil has a calming effect on the digestive system and can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, making it easier to digest food and reducing the risk of acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhetic acid and licoricidin. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can lead to acid reflux. Glycyrrhetic acid also has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to heal and protect them from acid damage. Licoricidin has antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and calm the digestive system. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Heat the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until melted.
In a separate bowl, mix the Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and beeswax. Add the powdered mixture to the melted coconut oil and shea butter mixture.
Stir until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and add the peppermint essential oil. Let cool and harden completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is a bioactive constituent that helps to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to acid reflux. The compound also has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, gingerol has a natural antispasmodic effect, which helps to calm down the spasms in the digestive tract that can cause acid reflux. The combination of anti-inflammatory, relaxing, and antispasmodic properties in gingerol makes Zingiber officinale creams a potential remedy for acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil, 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in ginger essential oil and aloe vera gel.
Let the mixture cool and thicken for about 30 minutes, then pour into small containers.
Let the creams cool and harden completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains essential oils, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation in the stomach, making it easier to swallow and relieving symptoms of acid reflux. The anxiolytic properties of menthol and menthone also help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate acid reflux. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of limonene help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing spasms and contractions that can contribute to acid reflux. By soothing the digestive system and reducing inflammation, Mentha x piperita creams can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and a few drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Add beeswax and stir until it melts completely. Remove from heat.
Add peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until it solidifies.
Once the mixture is solid, it's ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed to help soothe and calm acid reflux symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains the bioactive constituents anethole, limonene, and fenchone. These compounds have carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate pressure in the digestive system, a major contributor to acid reflux. Anethole, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause acid reflux. Fenchone's ability to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract can also help to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. By addressing the root causes of acid reflux, Foeniculum vulgare creams may provide relief from symptoms such as heartburn and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare (fennel seeds), 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare to the melted mixture and steep for 2 hours or overnight to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in the vitamin E oil and pour the cream into small containers. Let it cool and harden before use as a topical treatment for acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is made up of several bioactive constituents, including curcuminoids, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. Curcumin also has a protective effect on the lining of the stomach, preventing acid from leaking out and causing irritation. Additionally, curcumin has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the esophagus, reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, curcuma longa creams can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the turmeric powder until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed for acid reflux relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a high concentration of active constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, a common cause of acid reflux. Aloe-emodin, in particular, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and discomfort. Acemannan, a polysaccharide found in aloe vera, has been found to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing acid from damaging the lining and reducing the risk of acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, aloe barbadensis creams can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
Add melted coconut oil and shea butter to Aloe barbadensis gel and mix well.
Add beeswax to the mixture and heat until melted. Let it cool and thicken for 10-15 minutes.
Add peppermint essential oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is responsible for its therapeutic properties. This compound has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. Cinnamomum verum also contains eugenol, a natural antiseptic that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The oils present in Cinnamomum verum creams, such as cinnamon bark oil, have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract, Cinnamomum verum creams can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum verum essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and add the Cinnamomum verum essential oil. Stir well and let the cream cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are its active constituents. These compounds help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, preventing the backflow of stomach acid. The flavonoids also have antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by acid reflux. The terpenoids can help improve blood flow to the stomach, reducing the risk of acid reflux symptoms. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and olive oil to the melted mixture. Stir until combined and smooth.
Add dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids and let the cream cool before applying to affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains quinine, a key alkaloid that helps reduce acid reflux symptoms. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophagus and alleviate irritation caused by acid reflux. Cinchona officinalis creams also contain other alkaloids like cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have antacid properties that neutralize stomach acid and prevent it from flowing back up into the esophagus. The anti-inflammatory and antacid properties of these alkaloids help protect the esophagus from acid damage and reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes. The alkaloids in Cinchona officinalis creams also have a calming effect on the digestive system, promoting a more balanced and regular digestive process.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the Cinchona bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the ground Cinchona powder to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. The herb also has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing acid reflux symptoms. Melissa officinalis has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, acting as a natural protective barrier against stomach acid. Additionally, the herb has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the esophagus, reducing discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux. By applying Melissa officinalis creams topically, you can target the affected areas and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Melissa officinalis leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Melissa officinalis leaves to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common cause of acid reflux. The antispasmodic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate can also help alleviate stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, the calming effects of these constituents can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia creams may provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for acid reflux
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia, and a glass jar.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan until it reaches 100°F to 110°F (38°C to 43°C).
Steep the dried Lavandula angustifolia in the hot water for 10 to 15 minutes, then strain the mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter. Melt the mixture in a double boiler.
Combine the cooled Lavandula angustifolia mixture with the melted coconut oil mixture, and stir until well combined. Pour into a glass jar and let cool before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent acid reflux by soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation. Ginger and peppermint teas are known to calm the digestive system, while licorice root tea can help heal stomach ulcers. These teas can also relax the muscles in the esophagus, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help soothe acid reflux symptoms, but consuming them daily is a personal choice. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that may calm heartburn. However, others might irritate the stomach or interact with medications. Listen to your body and adjust your tea consumption based on how you feel.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms, but the time it takes to notice results varies. Some people experience relief after just one cup, while others may need to drink herbal teas consistently for a few days to a week to see an improvement.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for acid reflux in the evening can help.  The stomach is less active at this time, so the tea may work better. It's also a good idea to drink it after meals to help with digestion. This can make it easier to relax and get a good night's sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for acid reflux. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can slow down digestion, which can make acid reflux symptoms worse. Others, like ginger, can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding when taken with certain medications. This interaction can lead to increased side effects or reduced effectiveness of the medication.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's essential to be cautious about what you drink. Herbal teas can be a good choice for acid reflux, but some may not be safe. Peppermint and ginger teas are often recommended, as they can help soothe the stomach. However, other herbs like pennyroyal and sage can be too strong and might cause problems.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for acid reflux, but it's essential to choose teas that are gentle on the stomach. Peppermint and chamomile teas are popular choices, but some herbs like ginger and licorice root might not be suitable while breastfeeding. Always check the ingredients and potential effects on milk supply before consumption.
title_2: Acid Reflux in Adults: 11 Herbal Creams to Try
